title: Obligations of Filipino Seafarers. It is the duty of all seafarers to conduct
content: 59 themselves in the most professional, responsible, and ethical manner in the performance 60 of their duties and fulfill the following obligations: 61 59 A. DUTY TO FAMILY: 1 2 1) To provide financial and moral support to the seafarer’s family in the Philippines; 3 and 4 5 2) To communicate with the seafarer’s family as often as possible. 6 7 B. DUTY TO FELLOW SEAFARER: 8 9 1) To assist and cooperate with other crew on board the same ship; and 10 11 2) To conduct themselves professionally and refrain from engaging in and/or 12 encouraging any acts of harassment and bullying against their fellow crew or their 13 superior on board ships or onshore. 14 15 C. DUTY TO COUNTRY: 16 17 1) To uphold the ideals of the Republic of the Philippines and to defend it, if warranted; 18 19 2) To abide by the rules and regulations aimed at promoting the seafarer’s interest 20 and enhancing national gains; and 21 22 3) To be ambassadors of goodwill for the Philippines, demonstrating the best traits of 23 the Filipinos and refraining from any acts that may tarnish the Filipino image 24 abroad. 25 26 D. DUTY TO MANNING AGENCY AND/OR PRINCIPAL/EMPLOYER: 27 28 1) To provide the correct and true statements/certifications regarding the seafarer’s 29 skill, experience, and other qualifications; 30 31 2) To abide by the terms and conditions of the employment contract; 32 33 3) To maintain a high level of productivity as well as abide by company rules and 34 regulations; and 35 36 4) To refrain from committing acts which are detrimental to the interest of the 37 seafarer’s principal, employer, or LMA once a contract has been executed by the 38 seafarer and/or during the seafarer’s employment. 39 40 E. DUTY TO PORT STATE AND FLAG STATE: 41 42 To respect and obey the laws, morals, customs, and traditions of the port state and the 43 flag state. 44 45 46 INCENTIVES AND AWARDS 47 48
